hard.next_step: Thomas will speak internally since Ed has already sent across the deck to him and depending on that discussion, they would be open to get started with an ANA pilot project to understand it's capabilities
hard.ais_why: The proposed next step is an AI pilot: a four-week pilot for the Autonomous Negotiation Agent (ANA) to prove tail-spend savings. The 5 May session centered on Zycus AI (Merlin Intake conversational requisitions via MS Teams, iAnalyze real-time spend classification, a touchless invoicing agent, supplier-risk scraping). But customer-side enthusiasm is not yet strongly voiced and nothing is committed: no agreed pilot, POC, or budget. Qualified at 10 percent.
hard.competitor: SAP Ariba (incumbent); iRequest; Supplyhive; third-party spend-analysis vendor; do-nothing / status quo

north_star_verdict.headline: Strong first overview with Tom McNulty on 5 May, but he no-showed the 16 Jun follow-up and has not answered rep outreach since, leaving a single-threaded, informational deal with no economic buyer and no compelling event.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 8 May 2026, close 1 May 2027 = 358-day cycle, credible for a 12-15 month enterprise source-to-pay platform sale. About 299 days to close. Stage Qualified (discovery only), qualified 29 May. Last genuine two-way buyer engagement was the 5 May discovery call; the 16 Jun follow-up was a buyer no-show, and 9/15/16 Jun outreach drew no reply. That is roughly two months of rep-initiated chasing with no buyer response: momentum has stalled but the relationship is not explicitly dead (Tom promised to socialize the deck and is open to an ANA pilot). To reach Shortlisted by ~Oct 2026 for a credible May 2027 close, the deal needs re-engagement, multi-threading, and an economic buyer mapped now; at the current single-thread pace that velocity is not yet in place.
evidence: Last two-way buyer engagement was the 5 May discovery call; every touch since (9, 15, 16 Jun email/call attempts) is rep-initiated with no buyer reply (source: Salesforce activities May-Jun 2026, Avoma call history).

Tom McNulty no-showed the 16 Jun follow-up; on the internal Avoma call Ed said 'he's just busy or he's lost interest... he was very interested in the first meeting' and Vinay's live call attempt went unanswered (source: Avoma internal call 16 Jun 2026).

No economic buyer or decision maker is engaged: PSEG's CPO and CFO were only referenced by Ed from PSEG's 10-K, never met, and Tom is an operational Procurement Director without commercial authority for a $110k multi-year platform (source: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026).

No compelling event locked: Tom said 'this is informational, I didn't start with a specific need in mind'; a broader SAP modernization was mentioned but with no Ariba timeline or mandate (source: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026).

Stage still Qualified (discovery) with no RFI/RFP, no multi-person demo booked, and no exec sponsor, which is behind the pace needed to reach Shortlisted by Oct 2026 (source: Salesforce StageName, activities).

metrics.narrative: Tom McNulty articulated a specific, unquantified operational pain on the 5 May discovery call: PSEG cannot reliably differentiate gas vs electric construction spend because 'a requisitioner can buy the same thing 10 times and maybe call it 10 different things,' so spend is hard to categorize accurately. He confirmed PSEG runs roughly $4-6bn in annual procurement spend and outsources spend classification to a third party with a one-month lag ('the data is always a month behind'). No cost of misclassification, FTE burden, or savings target was ever put on the table, and Tom deferred detail questions to a solution consultant. The financial case to justify a $110k platform is therefore not built; quantifying the classification-accuracy gap and the ANA tail-spend savings (Ed cited 1-3 percent on tail spend) is the missing move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026, Tom McNulty: '$4bn to $6bn in annual procurement spend'; 'a requisitioner can buy the same thing 10 times and maybe call it 10 different things'; 'we use a third party to do our spend analysis... the data is always a month behind.'

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. The only PSEG person on the deal is Tom McNulty, a Procurement Director for electric/gas construction contracts, who owns the operational problem but not the budget for a multi-year platform. The CPO and CFO exist only as roles Ed inferred from PSEG's annual report and 10-K ('I pulled out some of the challenges... from your CPO and CFO'), not as named, engaged PSEG contacts. Budget authority for $110k of new source-to-pay software almost certainly sits with a CPO or Head of Strategic Sourcing plus finance sign-off, but none has been introduced. Mapping and reaching that budget owner, via Tom or a fresh multi-thread, is the single biggest gate to converting this from informational to funded.
sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: no roles; Tom McNulty is the sole task/activity contact.
Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026, Ed Dlugosz referencing PSEG's CPO/CFO priorities from public filings, not named contacts.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria surfaced organically from Tom's questions on 5 May rather than a formal evaluation, so they are inferred, not documented. Tom probed hardest on: real-time, accurate spend classification for dual gas/electric utility taxonomy (vs the current month-lagged third party); native single-platform architecture vs bolt-ons ('is your CLM homegrown, built from scratch?'); AI that can reach outside a closed system for sourcing help; and displacing/integrating with the SAP Ariba and iRequest stack. He also cared about supplier performance and safety/compliance for contractors ('add supplier performance to the left column'). No weighting, no scorecard, and no RFP have been established, so these are directional criteria a fit session would need to confirm.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026, Tom McNulty: 'CLM in your system, is that homegrown, built from scratch?'; 'I want my spend accurately categorized for gas construction'; 'add supplier performance to the left side of your column.'

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is unknown. Reading the call sequence as the process map, only one PSEG person has ever appeared (Tom, solo, on 5 May), he committed to 'socialize the deck with a few people internally,' then no-showed the 16 Jun follow-up and went silent, so no internal review, no committee, and no evaluation stages are visible. The one process clue is Tom's mention of an ongoing 'SAP modernization' that he assumed would include Ariba, which could become the forcing function for a formal look. What would create visibility is Tom naming who he is socializing to and whether a formal S2P evaluation or RFI is being scoped alongside the SAP modernization.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026, Tom McNulty: 'send me the deck and I'll socialize it with a few people'; 'we're going through an SAP modernization... I'm assuming that's gonna include Ariba.'
Avoma internal call 16 Jun 2026: Tom no-show, no reschedule.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been touched: this is a Qualified discovery deal with no pricing, no MSA/SOW, no legal, procurement, or InfoSec activity. The only paper-relevant color is that PSEG prefers its own contract paper ('we use our own paper to the max possible'), which is favorable for eventual redlining, and that Zycus's typical implementation runs 6-9 months after signature, per Ed. A no-cost 4-week ANA pilot was offered as a low-friction commercial entry point that sidesteps procurement early. Visibility here will only come once an economic buyer and a business case exist to trigger a formal evaluation.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026, Tom McNulty: 'we use our own paper to the max possible'; Ed Dlugosz: 'implementation takes 6 to 8 months, sometimes 9 months after contracts are signed.'

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed pain, articulated by the pain owner Tom McNulty on 5 May: inaccurate, delayed spend classification for a dual gas/electric utility, worsened by outsourcing analysis to a third party with a one-month lag, and difficulty ensuring requisitioners categorize spend correctly at the point of purchase. He also confirmed a stack of bolt-on tools (SAP Ariba, iRequest for CLM, Supplyhive for SPM, a third-party analytics vendor) that do not interoperate. Inferred pain, implied by the stack and Ed's narrative but not stated by Tom: tail-spend leakage that ANA could recover, occasional-user adoption friction, and supplier-risk blind spots (Tom's Colombia transformer flooding anecdote showed real appetite for proactive risk alerts). The pain is genuine but not yet owned above Tom or quantified.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026, Tom McNulty on spend nomenclature, third-party lag, and the transformer-flooding risk anecdote.

champion.narrative: Tom McNulty (Procurement Director, electric/gas construction) is the sole and developing champion. Advocacy shown on 5 May: he engaged deeply for over an hour, granted extra time, asked detailed platform questions, and committed to 'socialize the deck with a few people internally,' signaling intent to sell inward. What is missing and why he rates developing-to-weak: he has introduced no additional stakeholder in two months, has not reported any socialization outcome, no-showed the 16 Jun follow-up, and has not answered Ed's or Vinay's outreach since, so his engagement is cooling and unproven above his own desk. He has no demonstrated access to the CFO/CPO economic buyer. Re-securing Tom, or finding a second thread if he stays dark, is the priority.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026 (engaged, committed to socialize); Avoma internal call 16 Jun 2026 (no-show); Salesforce activities 9-16 Jun 2026 (no buyer reply); OpportunityContactRole (no roles).

competition.narrative: The incumbent and primary competitor is SAP Ariba, embedded because SAP is PSEG's ERP; Tom named it directly ('Ariba's the Ariba portion of SAP is really our direct competitor'). PSEG also runs iRequest for CLM (recently adopted, replacing SAP CLN) and Supplyhive for supplier performance (newly adopted), plus a third-party spend-analysis vendor. Zycus's wedge, positioned by Ed, is a single organically-built S2P platform vs Ariba's bolt-on architecture, no supplier fees, utility-specific classification via iAnalyze/Autoclass, and agentic AI (Merlin Intake over MS Teams, ANA tail-spend negotiation). The largest true competitor, though, is do-nothing inertia: Tom framed the whole engagement as informational with no specific need, so status quo wins unless a compelling event (the SAP modernization) and an economic buyer are activated.
sources: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026, Tom McNulty: 'SAP's our ERP... Ariba is really our direct competitor'; 'we moved from CLN to iRequest'; 'we have Supplyhive for our SPM.'

deal_movement.summary: Momentum peaked at the 5 May discovery call, then decayed: Tom no-showed the 16 Jun follow-up and stopped responding to rep outreach, leaving the deck 'sent and being socialized' but with no visible internal traction and no next milestone booked with a buyer present.

items.change: Strong 5 May discovery/overview call: Tom engaged for 60+ minutes, committed to socialize the deck internally, expressed interest in a no-cost 4-week ANA pilot.
Stage moved Initial Interest -> Qualified; Type set to New Business; source set to Influ2.
Tom no-showed the scheduled 16 Jun follow-up; live call attempt unanswered; Ed noted possible loss of interest.
Rep-initiated re-engagement attempts (9, 15, 16 Jun) via Clari email/call with no buyer reply.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the entrenched incumbent (Ariba runs on PSEG's SAP ERP) and the primary competitor, though Tom voiced no loyalty to it and even flagged its bolt-on CLM weakness. iRequest (recently adopted for CLM) and Supplyhive (recently adopted for SPM) are sticky recent bets that raise change fatigue, plus an unnamed third-party spend-analysis vendor. The single strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing / status quo: Tom explicitly called the engagement informational with no specific need, and he has gone quiet, so inertia beats us unless the SAP modernization becomes a forcing event and an economic buyer is engaged. No head-to-head evaluation is running.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo
competitors.quote: Tom McNulty: 'this is informational, I didn't start with a specific need in mind'; Ed on 16 Jun: 'they take the first call, all excited, and then you never see them again.'
competitors.how_we_win: Convert the SAP modernization into a compelling event and land a quantified ANA tail-spend pilot so inaction has a visible cost.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Tom McNulty: 'SAP's our ERP, so Ariba, the Ariba portion of SAP, is really our direct competitor.'
competitors.how_we_win: Position one organically-built S2P suite vs Ariba's bolt-ons, no supplier fees, and utility-grade classification; time the displacement to the Ariba license/SAP-modernization window.

competitors.name: iRequest
competitors.quote: Tom McNulty: 'we moved away from CLN to another product called iRequest' for contract management.
competitors.how_we_win: Offer to integrate to iRequest rather than force a rip-and-replace, then show unified sourcing-to-contract data flow iRequest cannot match as a single-vendor CLM.

competitors.name: Supplyhive
competitors.quote: Tom McNulty: 'we have Supplyhive for our SPM... we just started using it.'
competitors.how_we_win: Show native supplier performance built into the same platform as sourcing/contracts, removing another bolt-on and integration burden.

competitors.name: Third-party spend-analysis vendor
competitors.quote: Tom McNulty: 'we use a third party to do our spend analysis... the data is always a month behind.'
competitors.how_we_win: Displace the month-lagged external analysis with real-time iAnalyze/Autoclass classification inside the platform, tied to gas/electric taxonomy accuracy.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus fits PSEG's stated needs on paper (unified S2P, utility spend classification, agentic AI, supplier risk), but every fit point is Zycus-asserted from one demo, none validated by the buyer, and the economic case is unbuilt.

items.criterion: Accurate, real-time gas vs electric spend classification
items.quote: Tom: 'I want my spend accurately categorized for gas construction... the data is always a month behind' with the current third party.

items.criterion: Single organically-built platform vs bolt-on stack
items.quote: Tom: 'CLM in your system, is that homegrown, built from scratch?' Ed: 'built from scratch... one organic platform.'

items.criterion: Agentic AI (Merlin Intake, ANA tail-spend negotiation)
items.quote: Ed proposed a no-cost 4-week ANA pilot; Tom asked 'tell me about the pilot' but committed to nothing.

items.criterion: Coexistence with SAP ERP and iRequest CLM
items.quote: Ed: 'you wouldn't have to move from iRequest, we could integrate to iRequest... we tie right into SAP just as Ariba does.'

items.requirement: Answer the size/scope of the AI classification nomenclature library for a utility of PSEG's size.
items.quote: Tom: 'how big is the library of nomenclature, of classifications?' Ed: 'I'm not sure I can answer that... we can ask the solution consultant.'

items.requirement: Provide a CLM comparison of Zycus vs iRequest (pros/cons if starting from scratch).
items.quote: Tom: 'tell me on CLM versus iRequest if I was starting from scratch.'

items.deliverable: Deliver the no-cost 4-week ANA (Autonomous Negotiation Agent) tail-spend pilot Ed offered Tom.
items.grounding_quote: Ed: 'we also have a pilot we will do for Anna... a 4-week pilot... we could do it pro bono... meant to do it before you buy.'

items.deliverable: Tom to socialize the deck internally and revert on interest in an ANA pilot.
items.grounding_quote: Next Step: 'Thomas will speak internally since Ed has already sent across the deck... they would be open to get started with an ANA pilot project.'

items.quote: No PSEG CFO/CPO engaged; Tom is an operational Procurement Director.

items.area: No compelling event or quantified business case
items.quote: Tom: 'this is informational, I didn't start with a specific need in mind.'

items.area: Single-threaded on one contact who has gone quiet
items.quote: Tom no-showed 16 Jun and has not replied to outreach since.
items.source: Avoma internal call 16 Jun 2026; Salesforce activities

best_practice_check.summary: This is an early, single-threaded, cooling discovery deal; the win levers are re-engaging Tom with a concrete reason to respond, converting the SAP modernization into a forcing event, and reaching a budget owner.
flags: Re-engage Tom with a specific, low-effort hook (the classification-library answer plus a dated ANA pilot outline) rather than another generic follow-up, since three unanswered chases signal the ask is too vague.
Anchor the deal to PSEG's SAP modernization as the compelling event, framing the Ariba displacement window, to convert an informational look into a timed evaluation.
Break single-threading: use Tom's 'socialize internally' commitment to earn an intro to a strategic-sourcing or finance leader who owns S2P budget, so the deal is not hostage to one cooling contact.

items.name: Thomas McNulty
items.title: Procurement Director (electric/gas construction contracts)
items.sentiment: positive on 5 May, now cooling after 16 Jun no-show and silence
items.risk: Sole thread and developing champion who has gone quiet; owns the operational pain but not budget authority, has introduced no other stakeholder, and if he stays dark the deal goes cold.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 5 May 2026; Salesforce task/activity contact

champion_strength.summary: Tom McNulty is a developing champion who engaged strongly on 5 May and committed to socialize the deck internally, but has not surfaced a socialization outcome, introduced any additional stakeholder, or responded since no-showing the 16 Jun follow-up. His advocacy is real but unproven above his own desk, and it is currently cooling.
champion_strength.source: Avoma 5 May 2026 (engaged) and 16 Jun 2026 (no-show); Salesforce activities 9-16 Jun (no reply); OpportunityContactRole (none)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus's agentic AI story (Merlin Intake over MS Teams, ANA tail-spend negotiation, iAnalyze/Autoclass real-time classification, supplier-risk scraping) was central to the 5 May demo and maps well to PSEG's classification and tail-spend pain; the narrative landed as interesting but not yet as a committed pilot.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 (Moderate). The proposed next step is an AI pilot (4-week ANA), and Tom engaged on AI classification and touchless invoicing, but expressed no strong AI-forward mandate and committed to nothing, so appetite is present but unconfirmed.

items.detail: No economic buyer or exec sponsor engaged; deal cannot fund without CPO/CFO access.
Single-threaded on one Procurement Director who has stopped responding.
No compelling event or quantified ROI to justify $110k multi-year spend; do-nothing is the default.
PSEG recently adopted iRequest (CLM) and Supplyhive (SPM); change fatigue on top of SAP Ariba raises switching resistance.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: one strong buyer call, then a no-show and roughly two months of unanswered rep outreach. The deck is out and being socialized, but no buyer-side signal has returned.
items: Buyer no-show on 16 Jun follow-up (Avoma 16 Jun 2026).
No buyer reply to 9/15/16 Jun outreach (Salesforce activities).
No additional stakeholder introduced in two months (OpportunityContactRole).

items.action: Email Tom a two-line value hook: the classification-library answer plus a dated ANA pilot outline.
items.trigger: Tom no-showed 16 Jun and has not replied to three generic follow-ups; a specific, low-effort ask is needed to restart the thread.
items.expected_effect: Re-opens the cooling thread with a concrete reason to respond, testing whether Tom is still live before escalating.

items.action: Ask Tom to name who he socialized the deck to and request a warm intro to the S2P budget owner.
items.trigger: Tom committed on 5 May to 'socialize the deck with a few people internally' but no outcome or second stakeholder has surfaced.
items.expected_effect: Breaks single-threading and begins mapping the economic buyer, the gate to a funded evaluation.

items.action: Tie the pitch to PSEG's SAP modernization and scope the Ariba displacement window with a quantified tail-spend case.
items.trigger: Tom said 'we're going through an SAP modernization... I'm assuming that's gonna include Ariba,' a potential compelling event not yet activated.
items.expected_effect: Converts an informational look into a timed evaluation and builds the ROI needed to reach the economic buyer.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: PSEG is an early, single-threaded discovery deal: Tom McNulty ran a strong 5 May overview and promised to socialize the deck, but he no-showed the 16 Jun follow-up and has gone quiet, with no economic buyer engaged, no compelling event, and only an informational frame.

win_position.text: Zycus ran a strong first overview on 5 May: Tom McNulty engaged for over an hour on spend classification, single-platform architecture, and the ANA tail-spend pilot, and named SAP Ariba as the incumbent to displace (Avoma, 5 May).
No economic buyer is engaged: PSEG's CFO/CPO were only referenced from public filings, never met, so $110k of multi-year platform spend has no budget owner behind it (Avoma 5 May; no contact roles).
Tom framed the whole engagement as 'informational, I didn't start with a specific need in mind' and no compelling event is locked, so do-nothing inertia is the likeliest outcome (Avoma, 5 May).

deal_momentum.text: Tom no-showed the 16 Jun follow-up and did not answer Ed or Vinay; Ed's read was 'they take the first call, all excited, and then you never see them again' (Avoma, 16 Jun).
Since 5 May the only activity is rep-initiated chasing (9/15/16 Jun emails and call attempts) with no buyer reply, so the thread is cooling (Salesforce activities, Jun 2026).
A next step still exists: Tom is reviewing the socialized deck and is open to an ANA pilot, so the relationship is not dead if re-engaged well (Salesforce Next Step).

customer_commitment.text: Tom committed on 5 May to 'socialize the deck with a few people internally' and asked for detail on the no-cost 4-week ANA pilot (Avoma, 5 May).
That commitment has produced no visible outcome in over 60 days and no additional PSEG stakeholder has been introduced (Salesforce contact roles; activities).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on one operational Procurement Director with no exec access; if Tom stays silent the deal goes dark (Salesforce contact roles).
Incumbent SAP Ariba is embedded via the SAP ERP and a broader SAP modernization is underway that could either open or foreclose the replacement window (Avoma, 5 May).

deal_momentum.evidence: Buyer no-show 16 Jun plus two months of unanswered rep outreach; no buyer-initiated action since 5 May.

deal_risk.evidence: Single-thread on a cooling contact, no economic buyer, no compelling event.

customer_preference.evidence: Tom asked deep Zycus-specific questions on 5 May (homegrown CLM, real-time classification, ANA pilot) but stated no clear lean ('this is informational') and has since gone quiet.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain on spend-classification accuracy and third-party lag, plus a $4-6bn spend figure, but no quantified cost, savings target, or ROI.

scope_change.from: Full source-to-pay suite (iContract, iSource, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, ANA)
scope_change.to: Same suite, with a no-cost 4-week ANA pilot proposed as a low-friction entry point
scope_change.detail: No reduction versus a prior scope; the ANA pilot is a land-and-expand entry motion, not a narrowing of a previously larger deal.

calls_omitted.reason: No transcript or notes available (empty record).

gaps: No economic buyer or decision maker identified or engaged (knowledge gap).
No quantified business case or compelling event (knowledge gap).
Buyer has not responded since the 5 May call; internal socialization outcome unknown (knowledge gap).
